preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: growth-focused SaaS teams who need a semantic design layer that adapts to visitor intent
brief.goal: demonstrate MODE's ability to generate intent-aware pages at funnel scale

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Hero (slim) is required on every page including conversion — it provides a page title and immediate context so growth-focused SaaS teams understand they are on the right page before encountering the decision moment.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Conversion funnel stage dictates a stripped sequence: after the slim Hero, the Decision beat presents pricing and plan comparison immediately. This respects the Validator archetype's need for clear, structured information to evaluate before committing, while honoring the conversion-stage rule that only Hero → Decision → Conversion → Recovery beats are allowed.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Must appear immediately before Recovery per system rules. At the conversion stage this is the commitment prompt — the final CTA capture. For the Validator archetype, the preceding Decision beat supplies the proof-like structure (plan comparison, feature breakdowns) that satisfies their need for evidence within the constraints of the stripped conversion flow.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Always last. Provides secondary paths, footer links, and exit handling so Validators who aren't fully convinced have an alternative route rather than a dead end — preserving the relationship for organic re-engagement.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Hero (slim) is required on every page including conversion — it provides a page title and immediate context so growth-focused SaaS teams understand they are on the right page before encountering the decision moment.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Conversion funnel stage dictates a stripped sequence: after the slim Hero, the Decision beat presents pricing and plan comparison immediately. This respects the Validator archetype's need for clear, structured information to evaluate before committing, while honoring the conversion-stage rule that only Hero → Decision → Conversion → Recovery beats are allowed.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: Must appear immediately before Recovery per system rules. At the conversion stage this is the commitment prompt — the final CTA capture. For the Validator archetype, the preceding Decision beat supplies the proof-like structure (plan comparison, feature breakdowns) that satisfies their need for evidence within the constraints of the stripped conversion flow.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Conversion funnel stage requires minimal variant — the notes specify 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only' to avoid distraction from the decision moment below.

Conversion funnel stage paired with Validator archetype: text-only is specified for conversion as it 'orients the visitor without competing with the decision moment below.' Validator archetype would normally prefer social-proof variants, but conversion stage takes precedence per selection rules, and text-only maintains focus on the conversion decision.

slots.headline: The intelligence layer your design system is missing.
slots.subhead: MODE resolves component behavior, visual emphasis, and content register to the right experience for every archetype and funnel stage — without manual variants.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype in conversion stage requires cards that include 'description and longer feature list' to build trust through detail. highlighted variant surfaces the recommended plan prominently, supporting the Validator's need for trust signals and substantive product information.

slots.description: The complete semantic resolution engine — component manifest, palette map, IA planner, multi-variant generation, and Next.js preview UI. Yours forever, no recurring fees.
features: Semantic resolution engine — 4-dimension model: archetype, funnel stage, intent state, context mode
Component manifest — 11 components fully specified with zone affinity and slot schemas
IA planner — brief to full page architecture in seconds, powered by Claude Opus
Multi-variant generation — Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants from a single brief
Palette map — visual emphasis resolves per component per funnel stage automatically
Lifetime access — lives in your codebase, no runtime dependency, no lock-in

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires the variant that includes 'subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required')' — centered positioning with these elements reinforces credibility and removes friction objections common to Validator decision-making.

slots.headline: One purchase. A design system that finally knows why.
slots.subhead: MODE is operational in under an hour. Configure once, generate intent-aware pages at scale — no subscriptions, no vendor dependency.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. No subscription. Lifetime access.

page.reasoning: Conversion funnel stage on a key conversion page requires the with-cta variant which 'recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows' to maximize terminal conversion opportunities.
